source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: be properly inked in on durable paper or a polyester tracing film and be kept in good condition or in electronic form: Provided that where in a mine the plan is maintained in electronic form, the owner, agent and manager of the mine shall ensure that the plan is digitally signed and not liable to alteration or tampering and secured in such a manner as to facilitate retrieval by owner, agent, manager and the Chief Inspector of Mines or an Inspector;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: be accurate and maintained up-to-date not later than twelve months.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The plan required to be maintained under these regulations or any order made thereunder shall be kept available for inspection in the office of the mine and a list thereof shall be maintained.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: , by surveyor.- Every plan or section required to be maintained under these regulations or any orders made thereunder shall be prepared by or under the personal supervision of a surveyor and shall carry thereon a certificate by him to the effect that the plan or section or part thereof is correct and shall be signed and dated by the surveyor and counter-signed and dated by the manager of the mine on every occasion that the plan or section is brought up-to-date. CHAPTER – III INSPECTORS, MANAGEMENT AND COMPETENT PERSONS

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No person shall be appointed as Chief Inspector of Mines unless he holds a degree in mining engineering of an educational institution approved by the Central Government.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No person shall be appointed as Inspector of Mines unless he holds a degree in mining or petroleum engineering of an educational institution approved by the Central Government: Provided that -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In relation to electrical machinery installed in mines, the Central Government may appoint a person holding a degree in electrical engineering of an educational institution approved by it;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In relation to other machinery or mechanical appliance installed in mines, the Central Government may appoint a person holding a degree in mechanical engineering of an educational institution approved by it;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In relation to the provisions of the Act and of the rules and regulations which relate to matters concerning the health and welfare of persons, the Central Government may appoint a person holding a degree of bachelor of medicines and bachelor of surgery degree.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 12 - Definition of mine for purposes of this Chapter
text: For the purposes of this Chapter, all borings, boreholes, oil wells and accessory petroleum conditioning plants including gas wells, workshops, installations, and pipe conveying petroleum within an area duly demarcated by the owner or agent shall be deemed to be a mine: Provided that where special conditions exist, the Chief Inspector of Mines may, by an order in writing and subject to such conditions as he may specify therein, permit or require the division of any one such area into two or more separate mines.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner of a mine shall submit in writing to the Chief Inspector of Mines and the Regional Inspector, a statement showing names and designation of every person authorised to act on behalf of the owner in respect of management, control, supervision and direction of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The statement shall also show the responsibility of every person in respect of matters for which such person is authorised to act on behalf of the owner.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every such person shall be deemed to be agent for the mine or group of mines, as the case may be, in respect of the responsibility as specified in such statement.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The statement specified under sub-regulation (1) shall be submitted within one month from the date of opening or reopening of a mine or a group of mines, as the case may be:

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Provided that in case of a mine or group of mines, as the case may be, which has already been opened, such statement shall be submitted within a month of coming into force of these regulations.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Any change, addition or alteration in the names or other particulars of the statement referred to in sub-regulation (1), shall be reported in writing to the Chief Inspector of Mines and the Regional Inspector within fourteen days from the date of such change, addition or alteration.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 14 - Qualification and appointment of manager
text: No person shall open, work or re-open a mine unless there is a duly appointed manager of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 14 - Qualification and appointment of manager
text: No person shall act or continue to act, or be appointed as manager of a mine under sub- regulation (1), unless he holds a degree or diploma in engineering or post-graduation in physics or geophysics or geology or chemistry of an educational institution recognised by the Central Government and has experience in the management and supervision of operations in oil mines for a period of not less than fifteen years in case of diploma in engineering and ten years in other cases.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 14 - Qualification and appointment of manager
text: No person shall act or be appointed to act as manager of more than one mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 14 - Qualification and appointment of manager
text: Where by reason of absence or for any other reason, the manager of the mine is unable to perform his duties under the Act, and the regulations or bye-laws and orders made thereunder, the owner or agent of the mine shall authorise in writing a person possessing the qualifications under sub-regulation (2) to act as manager of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 14 - Qualification and appointment of manager
text: The person so authorised to act as manager of the mine shall during the period of such authorisation have the same responsibility, discharge the same duties, and be subject to the same liabilities as the manager of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 15 - Qualifications and appointment of deputy manager
text: At every mine having more than two installations, the owner, agent or manager of the mine shall appoint deputy manager at a scale of one deputy manager for every eight additional installations or part thereof, who shall assist the manager.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 15 - Qualifications and appointment of deputy manager
text: No person shall act or continue to act, or be appointed as deputy manager of a mine under sub- regulation (1), unless he holds a degree or diploma in engineering or post-graduation in physics or geophysics or geology or chemistry of an educational institution recognised by the Central Government and has experience in the management and supervision of operations in oil mines for a period of not less than eight years in case of diploma in engineering and five years in other cases.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 16 - Qualification and appointment of installation manager
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall ensure that every installation in the mine is placed under the charge of an installation manager appointed by him: Provided that, where in a mine, the owner, agent or manager of a mine is of the opinion that the size, nature, location and extent of certain installations are such that one installation manager can perform his duties in proper manner for more than one installation, he may appoint one installation manager to hold charge of more than one installation recording justifications thereof and clearly specifying the area of jurisdiction in every such case.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 16 - Qualification and appointment of installation manager
text: The Installation manager appointed under sub-regulation (1) shall hold a degree or diploma in engineering or a degree in science of an educational institution recognised by the Central Government and has experience in the management or supervision of operations in oil mines for a period of not less than five years in case of diploma in engineering and degree in science; and two years in case of degree in engineering.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 17 - Qualification and appointment of safety officer
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall appoint a safety officer to assist the manager of the mine in promotion of safety and occupational health at work: Provided that, where in a mine, the owner, agent or manager of the mine is of the opinion that, due to large size of the mine, or due to large number of installations in the mine, or due to other conditions existing in the mine, it is not possible for one safety officer to attend to his duties effectively by himself, the owner, agent or manager of the mine shall appoint such additional number of safety officers, clearly specifying their areas of jurisdiction, as may be adequate in carrying out duties effectively under these regulations.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 17 - Qualification and appointment of safety officer
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 73

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 17 - Qualification and appointment of safety officer
text: The safety officer appointed under sub-regulation (1) shall hold degree or diploma in engineering; or degree in science; or degree in industrial safety of an educational institution recognised by the Central Government or post-graduate diploma in industrial safety from Regional Labour Institute and has experience in the management or supervision of operations in oil mines for a period of not less than eight years in case of diploma in engineering and degree in science; and five years in other cases.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 18 - Qualification and appointment or authorisation of surveyors
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall appoint or authorize a person holding degree or diploma in mining or civil engineering of an educational institution recognised by the Central Government or certificate in surveying issued by Institution of Surveyors or surveyor’s certificate issued under the Act as surveyor to assist the manager of the mine as and when required and for carrying out the survey, levelling and for preparing plans and sections of the mine as required under these regulations and the orders made thereunder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 19 - Qualification and appointment of fire officer
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall appoint a fire officer for fire fighting and to assist the manager on fire prevention measures: Provided that, where in a mine, the owner, agent or manager of the mine is of the opinion that, due to large size of the mine, or due to large number of installations in the mine, or due to other conditions existing in the mine, it is not possible for one fire officer to attend to his duties effectively by himself, the owner, agent or manager of the mine shall appoint such additional number of fire officers, clearly specifying their areas of jurisdiction, as may be adequate in carrying out duties effectively.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 19 - Qualification and appointment of fire officer
text: No person shall be appointed as fire officer unless he:-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 19 - Qualification and appointment of fire officer
text: holds degree or diploma in fire and safety engineering of an educational institution recognized by the Central Government and having experience in dealing and management of fire services for a period of one year in case of degree or two years in case in diploma; or

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 19 - Qualification and appointment of fire officer
text: holds degree or diploma in engineering or degree in science and shall also have undergone divisional officers course of an educational institution recognised by the Central Government and having experience in dealing and management of fire services for a period of one year in case of degree or two years in case in diploma; or

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 19 - Qualification and appointment of fire officer
text: has undergone station officer’s or sub-officer’s course from an institute specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines for the purpose and having experience in dealing and management of fire services for a period of ten years.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 20 - Qualification and authorisation of competent persons for well perforation
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall authorise one or more persons to be competent persons for handling, transport and use of explosives meant for well perforation in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate standard, that is, OISD-STD-191 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 21 - Appointment of officials and competent persons
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall appoint such number of competent persons including officials, as is sufficient to secure, during each of the working shift,-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 21 - Appointment of officials and competent persons
text: adequate inspection of the installation and the equipment thereof;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 21 - Appointment of officials and competent persons
text: a thorough supervision of all operations at the installation;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 21 - Appointment of officials and competent persons
text: the installation, running and maintenance, in safe working order, of all machinery in the mine; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 21 - Appointment of officials and competent persons
text: the enforcement of the requirements of the Act and these regulations.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 21 - Appointment of officials and competent persons
text: The manager, deputy manager and installation manager of a mine shall ensure that the persons appointed under sub-regulation (1) are competent to perform duties assigned to them: Provided that no person shall be so appointed unless he is paid by the owner or agent and is answerable to the manager.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 21 - Appointment of officials and competent persons
text: The record of all appointments made under sub-regulation (1) and duties assigned to the competent persons under sub-regulation (2) shall be maintained by the manager of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall provide for the safety, health, welfare, environment and proper discipline of persons employed in the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Except in a case of emergency, no person who is not an official or a competent person shall give, otherwise than through the manager, instructions to a person employed in a mine, who is responsible to the manager. CHAPTER-IV DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF MANAGEMENT, CONTRACTORS, MANUFACTURERS, COMPETENT PERSONS AND WORKMEN

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: The owner of every mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: all provisions made under the regulations and rules pertaining to oil mines framed under the Act are complied with;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: all necessary measures are taken to eliminate or minimise the risks to health and safety of persons employed in mines;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: safety management plan required under these regulations are formulated and implemented in every mine;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: emergency plan specific to each mine required under regulation 102 is prepared for implementation in the event of an emergency;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: first aid, appropriate transportation from the workplace and access to appropriate medical facilities is provided to every person who receives injury or suffers from illness at the workplace;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: adequate training and re-training programs and comprehensible instructions on safety and health matters and on the work assigned are provided for workers, at no cost to them; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: all accidents and dangerous occurrences connected with mining operations are investigated and appropriate actions taken to prevent recurrence.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 23 - Duties and responsibilities of owner
text: Where persons engaged in any operation of a mine are likely to be exposed to physical, chemical or biological hazards, the owner of the mine shall ensure that such persons are provided, at no cost to them, with suitable protective equipment, clothing as necessary and other facilities as required by these regulations.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 24 - General responsibilities of supplier, manufacturer and designer
text: A person who designs, manufactures, imports, provides or transfers machinery, equipment or substances for use in mines, shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 24 - General responsibilities of supplier, manufacturer and designer
text: ensure that the machinery, equipment or substances do not entail dangers for the safety and health of those using them correctly; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 24 - General responsibilities of supplier, manufacturer and designer
text: make available-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 24 - General responsibilities of supplier, manufacturer and designer
text: information concerning their requirement for the correct installation, maintenance and use of machinery and equipment and the correct storage and use of substances;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 24 - General responsibilities of supplier, manufacturer and designer
text: information concerning the hazards of machinery and equipment, the dangerous properties of hazardous substances and physical agents or products; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 24 - General responsibilities of supplier, manufacturer and designer
text: information on how to eliminate or control risks arising from the identified hazards associated with the products.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A contractor deployed in a mine for any work shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: establish effective ongoing communication and co-ordination between appropriate levels of supervisors, officials and senior officials of the mine prior to commencing work, which shall include provisions for identifying hazards and the measures to eliminate and control risks;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure arrangements for reporting work related injuries and diseases, ill-health and incidents among the contractor’s workers while performing work in the mine;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 75

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: provide relevant workplace safety and health hazard awareness and training to their workers prior to commencing work and as the work progresses as necessary; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure compliance of the provisions of the Act and the rules and regulations framed thereunder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: When deploying contractors, the owner, agent and manager of every mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the same safety and training requirements apply to the contractors and their workers as to the workers of the establishment;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: where required, only such contractors are deployed who have been duly registered or held licenses; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the contract specify safety and health requirements and sanctions and penalties in case of non- compliance and such contract shall include the right for mine officials to stop the work whenever a risk of injury is apparent and to suspend operations until the necessary remedies have been put in place.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: Every person employed in a mine shall comply with the provisions of the Act and of the regulations and orders made thereunder and to any order or direction issued by the manager or an official of the mine with a view to the safety or convenience of persons, not being inconsistent with the Act and these regulations; and he shall not neglect or refuse to obey such orders or directions.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: Every person shall comply with the safety directives and be conversant with the safety instructions issued from time to time by the manager, deputy manager or installation manager of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: Before beginning work, every person shall examine his place of work and the equipment that he is to use and shall forthwith report to his superior any dangerous defect that he may discover.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: Every person shall make use of all safeguards, safety devices and other appliances provided for his protection or the protection of others.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: Except in an emergency, no person unless duly authorised shall interfere with, remove, alter or displace any safety device or other appliance provided for his protection or the protection of others or interfere with any method or process adopted with a view to avoid accidents and injuries to health.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: No person shall, while on duty-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: throw any stone or other missile with intent to cause injury; or

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: fight or behave in a violent manner; or

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: impede or obstruct any other person in the discharge of duties; or

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: offer or render any service or use any threat, to any other person with a view to prevent the other person from complying with the provisions of the Act or the regulations, or orders made thereunder or from performing duties faithfully.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: No person shall sleep or take rest in a dangerous place such as scaffolds, derrick floor or cranes or in the vicinity of dangerous or toxic substances, machinery, boilers, vehicles and heavy equipment.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: Every person shall wear protective equipment and clothing suited to his duties and to the weather conditions.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: Every person receiving any injury or notice any injury in the course of his duty shall, as soon as possible, report the same to an official or the competent person in-charge of a first-aid station, who shall arrange for the necessary first-aid to the injured person.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 26 - Duty of persons employed in mine
text: No person shall, except with the authority of an official, remove or pass through any fence, guard barrier or gate, or remove any danger signal.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of every mine shall be responsible for overall management, control, supervision and direction of the mine to ensure that the work is carried on in accordance with the provisions of the Act and of the regulations and orders made thereunder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of every mine shall ensure that sufficient supply of materials and appliances for the purpose of carrying out the provisions of the Act, the regulations and orders made thereunder and for ensuring the safety of the mine and persons employed therein, is always provided at the mine; and if he is not the owner or agent of the mine, he shall report in writing to the owner or agent of the mine when anything which he is not competent to order, is required for the aforesaid purpose and a copy of every such report shall be maintained.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of a mine shall assign to every competent person and official his specific duty and on his appointment provide to him a copy of the regulations, rules and bye-laws and any orders made thereunder which affect him and he shall take all possible steps to ensure that every such person understands, carries out and enforces the provisions contained therein in a proper manner.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of a mine shall examine all reports, registers and other records required to be made or kept in pursuance of the Act, the regulations and orders made thereunder and shall countersign the same and date his signature: Provided that the manager may, by an order in writing, delegate this duty to a deputy manager except in cases where a specific provision is made requiring the manager to countersign a report or register.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of a mine shall pay attention to, and cause to be carefully investigated, any specific representation or complaint that may be made to him in writing by a work person of the mine as to any matter affecting the environment or safety or health of persons in or about the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: When an accident resulting in any serious bodily injury to any person or loss of life occurs in a mine, the manager shall inspect the site of accident immediately and shall also either himself or through safety officer or through an enquiry committee duly constituted by him have an enquiry made into the causes of, and circumstances leading to, the accident and the result of every such enquiry and a plan and section of the site of the accident showing the details shall be submitted to the Regional Inspector within thirty days of the date of occurrence.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of a mine shall perform such other duty as specified under the Act, the regulations and orders made thereunder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of a mine may suspend, or take such disciplinary action, or recommend as he thinks fit against, the work person for contravention of any provisions of the Act, or the regulations and orders made thereunder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of every mine shall frequently inspect the mine and maintain a record therein the findings of each of his inspections and also the action taken by him to rectify the defects mentioned, if any.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 27 - Duty and Responsibility of manager
text: The manager of a mine shall identify operations of hazardous nature and ensure framing and usage of code of practice to be followed by persons engaged in the operation which shall be consistent with the Act and the regulations or orders made thereunder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 28 - Duty and Responsibility of deputy manager
text: The deputy manager of a mine shall carry out the duties assigned to him by the manager of the mine, and shall ensure that in the part of the mine or installations assigned to him by the manager, all work is carried out in accordance with the provisions of the Act and of the regulations or orders made thereunder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 28 - Duty and Responsibility of deputy manager
text: The deputy manager shall –

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 28 - Duty and Responsibility of deputy manager
text: ensure that all operations in the part of the mine assigned to him under sub-regulation (1) are carried out in accordance with the code of practice framed under these regulations;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 28 - Duty and Responsibility of deputy manager
text: subject to the orders of the manager, visit and examine the part of the mine and the installations under his charge or part thereof;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 28 - Duty and Responsibility of deputy manager
text: maintain a detailed report of the result of each of his inspection and also action taken by him to rectify the defects noticed, if any;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 28 - Duty and Responsibility of deputy manager
text: ensure that when any drilling rig, work-over rig and associated equipment, production equipment or pipeline is shifted or newly installed, it is given a trial run before being put into use; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 28 - Duty and Responsibility of deputy manager
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 77

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 28 - Duty and Responsibility of deputy manager
text: in the absence of manager, the deputy manager shall have the same responsibility and discharge the same duty and shall be subjected to same liabilities as manager, but not so as to exempt the manager therefrom.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: The installation manager of a mine shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: have charge and control of the installation and shall carry out such duties, as may be assigned to him by the manager or deputy manager;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: ensure that in the installation assigned to him, all work is carried out in accordance with the provisions of the Act and the regulations or the orders made thereunder;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: ensure that all operations in the installation or part of the mine assigned to him under clause (a) are carried out in accordance with the code of practice framed under these regulations;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: ensure that a notice of his appointment is available at site and his name is displayed at a place in the installation in such a position that it may be easily and conveniently read;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: visit and examine the installation or part thereof under his charge on every working day to see that safety in every respect is ensured;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: maintain a detailed record of the results of each of his inspection and also of the action taken by him to rectify the defects noticed, if any;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: ensure that when any drilling rig, work-over rig and associated equipment or production equipment or pipeline is shifted or newly installed, a trial-run is given under his supervision or person so authorised by him before it is put into use;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: ensure that all persons employed at the installation are thoroughly instructed and familiar with the provisions of the standing orders, standard operating procedures, code of practices and emergency plan made under these regulations relating to prevention of blowout and fire;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: ensure that the provisions of the Act and the regulations or orders made thereunder relating to the installation, maintenance, operation or examination of machinery and equipment are properly carried out by himself or by officials or by competent persons or work persons, as the case may be, appointed for the purpose; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 29 - Duty and responsibility of installation manager
text: during the construction of an installation or any operation thereat, when there is an emergency or apprehended emergency endangering the life or safety of any person or the stability and safety of the installation, himself take or cause to be taken such measures as are necessary or expedient to avoid the emergency.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The safety officer shall -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: inspect, as often as may be necessary, the installations of the mine with a view to identify the dangers which may cause bodily injury or impair health of any person or environment;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: advise the manager or deputy manager on measures necessary to prevent dangerous situations;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: enquire into the circumstances and causes of all accidents whether involving persons or not and advise the manager or deputy manager on measures necessary to prevent recurrence of such accidents;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: collect, compile and analyse information in respect of accidents and dangerous occurrences with a view to promote safe practices and improvement of working environment;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: organise regular safety education programmes and safety campaigns to promote safety awareness amongst persons employed in the mine;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that all new workers and workers transferred to new jobs receive adequate training in safety, firefighting and first aid; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: maintain a detailed record of work performed by him every day.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The safety officer shall be assisted by adequate number of assistants for the discharge of his duties.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No duty other than those specified in sub-regulation (1) shall be assigned to the safety officer.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The fire officer shall -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure the compliance of the provisions of the Act and the regulations or orders made thereunder concerning fire detection, fire-fighting system and emergency plan and advise the manager and deputy manager on measures necessary to ensure adequate protection against fire;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that code of practice for fire-fighting is prepared and implemented;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure proper layout, installation and maintenance of fire-fighting equipment;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that emergency plan for likely fire situations are prepared;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: organise regular training of persons in-charge of fire-fighting duty with particular reference to contingency or emergency plan for fire, correct assessment and handling of fire problem;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that persons on firefighting duty undertake simulated fire drills at least once in every month to study promptness of response and effective tactics;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: examine at least once in every quarter all devices and equipment of fire detection and fire- fighting systems in the mine and report any defects in the same to the manager or deputy manager;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: exercise a general supervision and co-ordination during control and extinguishment of any fire in the mine;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: assist the manager or other officials so authorised, to conduct enquiry into the causes and circumstances of all fires with a view to prevent recurrence in the mine; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: maintain detailed record of work performed by him every day.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No duty other than the duty specified in sub-regulation (1) shall be assigned to the fire officer.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 32 - Duty and responsibility of competent person for well perforation
text: The competent person appointed or authorised under sub-regulation (1) of regulation 20 shall be responsible, under these regulations or orders made thereunder, for handling, transport and use of explosives in the mine in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD-STD-191 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 32 - Duty and responsibility of competent person for well perforation
text: The competent person for well perforation shall –

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 32 - Duty and responsibility of competent person for well perforation
text: be responsible for the compliance by his assistants, if any, of such provisions and of any direction with a view to safety, which may be given to them by a superior official;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 32 - Duty and responsibility of competent person for well perforation
text: not hand over any explosive to any unauthorised person;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 32 - Duty and responsibility of competent person for well perforation
text: be present when shot is being charged and shall himself fire the shot; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 32 - Duty and responsibility of competent person for well perforation
text: be responsible when a shot has misfired to see that the gun is safely disarmed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 32 - Duty and responsibility of competent person for well perforation
text: The competent person shall maintain detailed record of the work performed by him.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every competent person shall be subject to the orders of superior officials and shall perform his duty assigned to him in accordance with the provisions of the Act and of the regulations or the orders made thereunder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The competent person shall not -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: depute another person to perform his work without the sanction of his superior official;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: absent himself without having previously obtained permission from such official for the period of his absence or without having been relieved by a duly competent person; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: without permission from such official, perform during his shift, any duties other than those for which he has been appointed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The competent person shall, on the occurrence at his place of work any hazardous condition, take prompt corrective measures to eliminate the hazard.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that every part of a derrick, mast and sub-structure is of sound construction and strength, meeting the maximum load criteria for which it is designed and shall be maintained in safe working condition all the time.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The derrick shall be adequately secured to prevent it from overturning.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall provide on every derrick or portable mast, a platform at least sixty centimetre wide on all sides of the crown block equipped on its outer edge with a two-rail railing at least one metre high and toe-board fifteen centimetre high.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall provide on every derrick, platform for persons to stand on while such persons handle pipe or other equipment racked in or on the derrick which shall cover the space from the working edge of the platform to the legs and girts of the derrick and be firmly secured.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The working edge of monkey board platform shall be so placed that there is adequate clearance for safe passage of travelling block.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The platform, floor and walkway shall be kept free of dangerous projection or obstruction and shall be so maintained that adequate protection against slipping is provided.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every derrick shall be equipped with a ladder arrangement ensuring safe access to the elevated walking and working platform.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Access from ladder to working platform shall be properly secured with railings and toe boards.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every ladder shall have rungs equally spaced and the top end of each ladder section shall extend not less than one metre above the platform.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall provide landing platform or cage on ladders of more than six metre to a maximum unbroken length of nine metre: Provided that nothing in this sub-regulation shall apply where personal fall arrest system is used on the ladder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The landing platform shall be equipped with railings and toe-boards so arranged as to give safe access to the ladder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The ladder leading from derrick platform to monkey board shall be provided with fall prevention device meeting with applicable Indian or international standard to prevent persons from falling.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 37 - Safety belt and life line
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall provide to every person who works above the first girt of a derrick, safety belt with full body harness and lifeline certified as per standard, that is, IS 3521 of the Bureau of Indian Standard or its revised version and he shall use the same unless otherwise protected against the danger of falling from height.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 37 - Safety belt and life line
text: No person shall work or travel from where he is likely to fall for more than one metre and eighty centimetre unless he is protected by certified safety belt with full body harness and lifeline and shall use the same whilst at work.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that an escape device with escape line and slide of adequate strength, as per the standard specified for the purpose by the Chief Inspector of Mines by a general or special order, is installed and maintained on every monkey board in such a manner that persons may come down safely from the monkey board to ground level in an emergency.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The escape line shall be securely fastened to the girt immediately above the monkey board and it shall be securely anchored to ground at a distance, from centre of cellar pit, not less than the height of the monkey board from the ground.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The landing place shall be provided with adequate quantity of sand or other suitable material for cushioning to prevent hard landing.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A competent person shall inspect every part of emergency escape device, braking system as per the inspection and maintenance guidelines of original equipment manufacturer and the record of every such inspection shall be maintained by the person who made the inspection.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The track rope of the emergency escape device shall have sufficient sag to avoid straining due to pre-tensioning.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The track rope or any part thereof shall have no damage or kink.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The braking unit pulleys of the emergency escape device shall be checked for free rotation prior to each installation and they shall be checked for any wear and cleaned to make it free from slippage.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall provide and use on every rig, a weight indicator to register a close indication of the load suspended from the casing line.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The weight indicator shall be maintained in working order and calibrated at intervals not exceeding six months.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The rig floor area and each draw-works engine floor area shall have not less than two escape exits placed on opposite sides to allow unobstructed escape.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that the floor openings and floor holes are guarded by a standard railing and toe-board or cover.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Where any person is allowed to work or pass through open-sided floor or platform of height one metre and eighty centimetre or more above the adjacent floor or ground level, the open-sided floor or platform shall be guarded by a standard railing.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that standard railing is provided on the inside of all mud tank runways, unless other means are available to prevent a person from falling into the mud tanks.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The open sided floor, walkway, platform or runway above or adjacent to dangerous equipment and similar hazards shall be guarded with a standard railing and toe-board.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the draw-works is fitted with a suitable device with its control near the driller’s stand to stop the draw-works in case of an emergency;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: no draw-works is operated unless all guards are in position and maintained;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the machinery is stopped for oiling and greasing if lubrication fittings are not accessible with guards in place;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the brakes, linkage and brake flanges of draw-works are examined by a competent person once at least in every twenty four hours and if any defect is found during such examination, the draw-works are not used until such defect is remedied;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the result of every such examination specified under clause (d)is recorded by the competent person making the inspection;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the draw-works are provided with an automatic device which shall effectively prevent the travelling block from coming closer than two metres of the crown block on the one end and crashing on the rotary table at the other end;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The cat-heads operated manually shall be equipped with a guide divider to ensure separation of the first wrap of line or rope.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The key seat and projecting key on a cat-head shall be covered with a smooth thimble or plate.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: When a cat-head is in use, a competent person shall be at the controls and in the event of any emergency, he shall immediately stop the rotation of the cat-head.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The operator of the cat-head shall keep his operating area clear and shall keep the portion of the cat-line not being used coiled or spooled.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 81

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The uncontrolled rotation of pipes shall be effectively prevented while making or breaking pipe connections and a back-up tong shall be used for this purpose whenever required.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The tong counter balance weights and lines shall be provided with guards to prevent accidental contact.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The ends of tong safety lines shall be secured with not less than three wire-line clamps, or crimped.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The tongs, ends of rotary hose and suspension sheaves shall be fitted with safety chains or wire lines.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The casing line shall be visually examined by a competent person once at least in every seven days and the condition of the wire as to wear, corrosion, brittleness and fracture shall be noted, and a report of every such examination shall be recorded by the person who made the examination.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: If during any inspection, any defect or weakness is found by which the safety of persons may be endangered, such weakness or defect shall be promptly reported in writing to the installation manager or manager and until such weakness or defect is remedied, the casing line shall not be used.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The slip and cut operation shall be carried out under the supervision of the installation manager or other competent person who shall record the date and other particulars thereof and shall sign with date.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD STD - 187 or its revised version for inspection, slip and cut procedure of casing line shall be complied with, so as to prevent excessive wear of the casing line.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 47 - Rigging equipment for material handling
text: The rigging equipment including cranes for material handling shall be checked prior to and during its use to ensure that it is safe.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 47 - Rigging equipment for material handling
text: The rigging equipment shall not be loaded in excess of its recommended safe working load.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 47 - Rigging equipment for material handling
text: While operating cranes in the vicinity of overhead electric transmission line, adequate precaution shall be taken against accidental contact with the electric transmission line unless the same is kept de- energised during movement of the crane.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 47 - Rigging equipment for material handling
text: The rigging equipment and crane shall be inspected by a competent person at such interval and in accordance with the procedure as laid down by the manager or installation manager of the mine and he shall record the results of the inspection.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The materials stored in tiers shall be stacked, racked or otherwise secured to prevent sliding, falling or collapse.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The passage way shall be kept clear to provide for the free and safe movement of material handling equipment or persons.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 49 - Construction and loading of pipe-racks
text: The construction of pipe racks shall be designed and maintained to support any load placed thereon.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 49 - Construction and loading of pipe-racks
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall make adequate provision to prevent pipe, tubular material or other round material from rolling off pipe-racks.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 49 - Construction and loading of pipe-racks
text: No person shall go or be allowed to go between pipe-rack and a load of pipe during loading, un- loading and transferring operations of pipes unless effective protection device or system is provided to protect such person from being hit by any load of pipe in motion.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the raising and installation of heavy load is performed during daylight hours unless adequate general lighting arrangement is provided at the place of work;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: every loose part and tool of the rig is securely fastened;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: guy lines, cat lines, snub lines and such other lines are not installed within six metre of any electric overhead transmission lines;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the exhaust of internal combustion engine is provided with water quench or other effective spark arrestors;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: high pressure circulating fluid line and steam line are securely anchored;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: during rigging-up and rig dismantling, the wellhead is protected against damage from sliding or falling objects of the rig and associated material;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the components from a loft including nuts, bolts and cleats are lowered safely to the ground either singly, bundled or in containers; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the code of practice for rigging-up and dismantling is framed and implemented and kept updated by a deputy manager or an installation manager authorised in this behalf.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of the mine shall provide and maintain mud tanks and mud pumps of sufficient capacity for effective control of the well.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the mud tanks are so designed and installed as to provide positive suction to mud pumps;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the mud pumps connected to a drilling rig are equipped with a pressure relief valve and an operating gauge in the system;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the pressure relief valve is set to discharge at a pressure not more than ten per cent in excess of the established maximum working pressure of the pump, pipes and fittings;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the discharge from a pressure relief valve is piped to such a place which does not endanger persons and the discharge line is anchored securely; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: there is no valve between a pump and its pressure relief valve.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: The safe practice for work-over operations, well stimulation operations and blowout prevention equipment systems for drilling wells shall be in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Guidelines, that is, OISD-GDN-182 and Oil Industry Safety Directorate Recommended Practices, that is, OISD-RP– 174 or their revised versions.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: In well control equipment and operations, the manager of the mine shall ensure that--

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: well control fluid and equipment are available and installed and used as required;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: well is full of well fluid of adequate specific gravity to overbalance the bottom hole pressure;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: during tripping operation, closely watch to notice any sign of kick in the well and annulus is kept full;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: full opening safety valve with connection for circulation and operating key are readily available and kept in open position on the derrick floor;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: blow out preventer and related equipment like choke and kill manifold, control panel and accumulator are available, maintained and tested regularly;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: blow out preventer equipment is function-tested and pressure-tested when installed and also at the appropriate time during operations to determine that the blow out preventer functions properly;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: instructions of the blow out preventer operating manual of the manufacturer are complied with;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: all connections, valves, fittings, piping, and other equipment have a minimum working pressure equal to the rated working pressure of the blow out preventer;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: bolts, nuts and fittings of correct size are used and tightened to the recommended torque; and all connections are pressure-tested before resuming operation;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: all manually operated valves are equipped with hand wheels, and always kept ready for use; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
text: pit drills are carried out regularly.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 52 - Well control equipment and operation
