source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 53 - Selection of well control equipment
text: The selection of well control equipment shall be made in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Recommended Practices, that is, OISD-RP–174 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 54 - Periodic inspection and maintenance of well control equipment
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 54 - Periodic inspection and maintenance of well control equipment
text: establish by taking into consideration the original equipment manufacturer’s recommendations for inspection and maintenance procedures of well control equipment; .

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 54 - Periodic inspection and maintenance of well control equipment
text: ensure that the well control equipment is cleaned, visually inspected and preventive maintenance performed, before installation at the next well after inspection of each well;.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 54 - Periodic inspection and maintenance of well control equipment
text: ensure that major inspection is carried out at intervals of not more than five years of service or as per Original Equipment Manufacturer’s recommendation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 54 - Periodic inspection and maintenance of well control equipment
text: ensure that the records of every inspection made under clauses(b) and (c) is maintained by the competent person who made the inspection.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 54 - Periodic inspection and maintenance of well control equipment
text: comply with the spare parts requirement as per Original Equipment Manufacturer;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 54 - Periodic inspection and maintenance of well control equipment
text: ensure that separate maintenance history or log book of the entire blow-out preventers, choke manifold and control units are maintained; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 54 - Periodic inspection and maintenance of well control equipment
text: ensure that all pressure gauges on the blowout preventers control system are calibrated at least once in every six months.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 55 - Control system for blowout preventers
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 55 - Control system for blowout preventers
text: all ram preventers have locking mechanism and instructions for operating the controls are posted prominently near the control unit;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 55 - Control system for blowout preventers
text: all controls of power operated blowout preventers are located within easy reach of the driller on the derrick floor;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 55 - Control system for blowout preventers
text: a remote control panel for the blowout preventer is installed at ground floor level at a safe distance from the derrick floor; .

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 55 - Control system for blowout preventers
text: blowout preventers are connected to an accumulator system which shall be capable of providing fluid of sufficient volume to close all the blowout preventers in the stack and open hydraulically operated valve; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 55 - Control system for blowout preventers
text: all controls for blowout preventers are clearly identified with suitable markers;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 56 - Testing of blowout preventer assembly
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that testing of blowout preventer assembly is conducted in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Recommended Practices, that is, OISD-RP– 174.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall ensure that the following control equipment for the drilling mud system are installed and kept in use during drilling operations, namely:-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a pit level indicator registering increase or reduction in the drilling mud volume and a visual and audio warning device near the driller’s stand;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a device to accurately measure the volume of mud required to keep the well filled at all times;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a gas detector or explosimeter at the primary shale shaker and connected to audible or visual alarm near the driller’s stand;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a device to ensure filling of well with mud when the string is being pulled out; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a control device near the driller’s stand to stop the mud pump when the well kicks.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: If the control equipment referred to in sub-regulation (1) indicates that formation fluid is entering the well, immediate steps shall be taken to control the well.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of every mine in which blow out preventer assembly is installed, shall, immediately after such installation, frame standing orders specifying the action to be taken when a well kicks and the duty of each person employed on the rig and of such other persons as may be necessary.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A copy of standing orders referred to in sub-regulation (3) shall be posted at conspicuous places near the rig.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every person employed on a rig shall have an adequate understanding of the warning sign of a kick, the standing orders mentioned under sub-regulation (3) and the blowout preventer assembly, and competent person on the rig shall have knowledge to operate the controls for blowout preventer; and blowout prevention drill shall be conducted for this purpose once in seven days.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Suitable control valves shall be kept available near the well which may be used in case of emergency to control the well.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: When running in or pulling out string, a suitable mechanism shall be kept readily available at the derrick floor to prevent uncontrolled flow from the string.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 58 - Precautions after occurrence of a blowout
text: On the appearance of signs indicating that a well is blowing out, the manager of the mine shall ensure that all persons other than those whose presence is deemed necessary for controlling blowout are immediately withdrawn from the installation and suitable action is taken in accordance with the procedures formulated in the emergency plan prepared under

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: During the whole time that any work of controlling a blowout is in progress, the manager of the mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a competent person is present on the spot throughout;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: an area within five hundred metre of the well on the down wind direction is demarcated as danger zone, and-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all electrical installations within the danger zone is de-energised to prevent the ignition;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: flameproof or intrinsically safe lamp or torch is only be used within the danger zone; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: no naked light or vehicular traffic is permitted within the danger zone;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a competent person ascertains the condition of ventilation and presence of gas with a flameproof or intrinsically safe instrument so far as safety of person is concerned;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate number of self-contained breathing apparatus or any other apparatus of such make in accordance with the standards, that is, IS 10245 Part-2 or IS 10245 Part 1 of the Bureau of Indian Standards or its revised versions are available at or near the place for use in emergency; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate fire-fighting facility is kept readily available for immediate use.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: At the beginning of every shift, the instruments and controls at the driller’s stand, draw-works, mud pumps, sensing and monitoring instruments, mud logging, casing line, cat-line and blowout preventer assembly shall be examined by the person in- charge of the shift or a competent person and he shall satisfy himself that they are in good working order.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of a mine shall ensure that a detail plan of operations relating to safety and geo- technical matters is formulated before the commencement of drilling operation or work-over plan for work- over operation for the individual well and the same is made available to installation managers, other concerned officials and competent person before the commencement of the said operation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Before commencement of drilling or work-over operation, a pre-spudding or pre-work-over meeting shall be carried out to examine and ensure safe operation of drilling or work-over and other related activities, and the finding shall be recorded and signed by the members.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 85

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The driller shall see that no person remains in a position of danger at or near the rotary table before the rotary table is set in motion.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The casing line shall not be in direct contact with any derrick member or foul with any material in the derrick excepting the crown block and any travelling block sheaves, a line spooler, a line stabilizer or weight indicator.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: When cementing, no person shall be allowed on the rig floor near the wellhead or near the cementing line and equipment except those actually engaged on the operation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All high-pressure pipes fitted with flexible points shall be suitably anchored and pressure tested before the commencement of cementing operation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: After the cementing operation and prior to perforation, casing shall be hermetically tested at a pressure at least seventy per cent of the casing burst pressure.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Tools or other materials shall not be carried up or down a ladder unless properly secured to the body leaving both hands free for climbing.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of the mine shall make risk assessment to fix the minimum distance between the well to be drilled and railway, public road, public works or other permanent structure not belonging to the owner: Provided that, no well shall be drilled within a distance of forty five metre of any railways, public road, public works or any other permanent surface structure not belonging to the owner. Explanation.- For the purposes of sub-regulation (10), the distance shall mean the shortest distance between the well and any railways, public road, public works or any other permanent surface structure not belonging to the owner measured in any direction on surface.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall ensure that prior to the commencement of drill stem test-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: pressure test and function test of the blowout preventer assembly is conducted;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: fire-fighting equipment is kept readily available for immediate use;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: no person other than those required for the test is admitted on the drilling floor;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the test line is securely anchored at each end and at each nine metre interval;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: thekelly hose is not used as part of the test line; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the test line and valves are examined by a competent person and no test is conducted if any defect is discovered until such defect is rectified.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No person shall conduct the initial opening of drill stem test tool except in daylight hours.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No drill pipe shall be pulled out when petroleum or natural gas has been recovered during a drill stem test, unless the well is properly killed and there is no possibility of petroleum or natural gas being present in the drill pipe.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The gas produced to the atmosphere during a drill stem test shall be burnt through a flare-line or burners.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: PRODUCTION

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No well perforation shall be carried out except in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD-STD-191 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Christmas tree and flow line including the associated fittings shall be tested by applying maximum pressure likely to be encountered and the result of such test be recorded before commencement of testing or activation of well.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The installation manager or an official shall perform well testing and activation under his direct supervision and he shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: flow-lines are firmly anchored to the ground;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the separator safety valve is in good working order and properly adjusted;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: hydro tested separator is used;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate fire-fighting equipment is readily available for immediate use; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate facility is provided to safely collect the well products in tanks.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The installation manager or official referred to in sub-regulation (2) shall ensure that well control procedure is made available to every person at the site and he has understood the same, considering all eventualities before commencement of well testing.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The well testing and activation shall not be carried out beyond day light hours or under conditions of lightning, thunder, high wind and heavy rain, except with the permission of the manager based on job safety analysis and subject to observance of the control measures and other conditions he may specify.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A record of every permission referred to in sub-regulation (4) along with sufficient justification shall be maintained.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Whenever the owner, agent or manager of a mine proposes to construct any new production facility or carry out material alterations at existing production facility, which covers quick production system, early production system, oil collection station, group gathering stations, gas collecting station, gas compressor plant, central tank farm, intermediate tank farm, water injection plant, polymer injection plant, in-situ combustion plant or any other important facility in oilfield within any mine, he shall ensure that -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the following particulars are shown on the surface plan and a copy thereof is kept at the facility -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the name and location of any other production facility connected with the facility;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the pipelines connected with the facility;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the name of each well connected to the facility;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the extent of the land over which right of use or right of way has been established; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: any railway, public road, public works, building or any other surface features lying within sixty metres of such facility;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the documents, in compliance to all relevant statutory requirement, safety standards and safety guidelines are made available at site;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the production facility where the quantity of petroleum gas or liquid stored or handled exceeds three hundred tones respectively, and one lakh tonnes, the safety report in the form and method specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines for the purpose are made available at site; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the detailed piping and instrumentation diagram of facility is maintained and kept updated and a copy of the same is kept available at the facility.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall ensure that the production facilities are of sound construction and required strength and designed, constructed, operated and maintained based on risk assessment.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every tank for the storage of petroleum in bulk shall be designed, constructed, operated and maintained in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD- STD-118 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The petroleum storage tanks shall be located in dyked enclosures with roads all around the enclosure and all dyked enclosures shall be provided with proper discharge system to prevent accumulation of oil or water in the enclosures.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 87

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The layout and design of dyked enclosures for petroleum storage tanks shall be in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD-STD-118 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall ensure that the design practice for the tanks used for storage of petroleum is in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Recommended Practices, that is, OISD-RP-108 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: The height of the storage tank shall not exceed one and half times its diameter or twenty metre, whichever is less.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: The piping from and to any tank located in a dyked enclosure shall not pass through any other dyked enclosure and the piping connected to tanks shall run directly to outside the dyke to the extent possible to minimise piping within the enclosures.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: The minimum distance between a tank shell and the inside toe of the dyke wall shall not be less than half the height of the tank.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: There shall be access on all four sides of each dyke area and roads shall be linked to minimise the effect if one road is cut off during fire.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: An air space of not less than five per cent of the total capacity of the tank shall be kept in each tank.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: Every tank after being installed or reinstalled and before being put in use, shall be hydro tested in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD-STD-129 or its revised version by a competent person so as to ensure that it is free from any leakage and is suitable for storage of petroleum.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: A report of such test shall be maintained in the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: Every storage tank including its roof and all metal connections shall be electrically connected with very low earth resistance system in accordance with the standard and procedure as per an Indian standard or an international standard adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards through harmonisation, specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines by a general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided that where no such standard exists, an international standard specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines on merit, by a general order notified in the Official Gazette, may be accepted: Provided further that any other international standard equivalent to the harmonised standard, specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines on merit, by a general order notified in the Official Gazette, may also be accepted: Provided also that such earth resistance system shall not be used with storage tank in the mine unless the same has been tested and passed the test as per the applicable standards and the manager of the mine has kept a record of the type, details of specification, reference of the particular standards, test criteria as per the standards and status of testing, place of testing, copies of test report and any other relevant details.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: The effectiveness of earthing shall be tested once in twelve months and the result of every such test shall be maintained.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: No person shall enter or be permitted to enter a tank for cleaning or maintenance unless work permit for confined space entry has been obtained or issued and the tank has been examined by a competent person and found to be gas-free.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: The agent or manager of a mine shall provide walkway with handrail on the roof of the tank to facilitate inspection and checking of vents, flame arrestor and other facilities so that movement of personnel on roof is safe.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: Every tank shall be painted and its numbering, safe filling height and reference height shall be painted on the tank to avoid operating errors.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 66 - General requirement for storage tank and dyked enclosures
text: No gauging or sampling of tank shall be undertaken during thunder or hail storms.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 67 - Safety precaution in oil dispatch pump area
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 67 - Safety precaution in oil dispatch pump area
text: all moving parts of a pump are adequately guarded;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

text: the engine exhaust is fixed with spark arrestor;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of every mine shall ensure that pressure gauge of suitable range and non-return valve are fitted on each inlet lines to the manifold.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of every mine shall ensure that in process area-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: discharge line of safety relief valve or pressure safety valve of the pressure vessel is connected to the flare through a common header;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all bath heaters, indirect heaters and flare line are provided with remote ignition system;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: water level is always above the level of fire tube in operational bath heaters;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: flame arrestor is in place in air intake line of the burners;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all vessels, equipment, bath heaters or indirect heaters handling petroleum are effectively earthed;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: insulating mat of standard, that is, IS 15652 of the Bureau of Indian Standards or its revised version shall be provided near the electrical control panels;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: records of vessels and testing of safety relief valve or pressure safety valve are inscribed on the vessels and on the safety relief valve and pressure safety valve.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In gas fired heaters, namely heater treaters, bath heaters and indirect heaters, the manager of the mine shall provide burner management system in case of new fired heaters which shall shut off gas supply to main and pilot burners in the event of pilot flame out and necessary provision of audible alarm on pilot flame out.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: For the existing gas fired heaters, there shall be regular monitoring of hydrocarbon gas and record maintained by a competent person authorised in this behalf.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Wherever generator set is used in a mine, the owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that the same is maintained and operated in accordance with the Central Electricity Authority (Measures relating to safety and electric supply) Regulations, 2010 and the noise and emission levels in the nearby area are maintained within the limits specified under the Environment (Protection) Rules,

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: The gas compressors shed or house shall be located at a safe distance from heaters in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD-STD- 118 or its revised version and shall have roofing and open from sides to avoid accumulation of heavier vapour or gas on the floor of compressor house.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: The compressor shed or house shall be located near the battery limits to facilitate ease in maintenance and operation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: The compressors in the compressor shed or house shall be fitted with-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: pressure relief valves on inlet and all stages to prevent pressure build up above the pre- determined set point;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: high discharge temperature shut down mechanism and high cooling water temperature switch fitted on cooling water return line to shut the compressor in the event of a fault;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: high inlet, inter stage and discharge pressures shut down mechanisms;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: low lube oil pressure shut down mechanism;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: low cooling water flow switch fitted to the cooling water return line to shut the compressor in the event of fault; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house

text: a remote isolation switch, wherever applicable, for emergency shutdown with manual reset at control panel.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: The safety devices under sub-regulation (3) shall be inter-coupled with automatically operated hooter or audio alarm for indication to the operator.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 71 - Gas compressor shed or house
text: The manager of every mine shall install adequate number of hydrocarbon gas detectors in gas compressor shed or house inter-coupled with audio alarm system which triggers at twenty per cent of lower explosive limit.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 72 - General safety for production facility and installation
text: The manager of every mine shall -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 72 - General safety for production facility and installation
text: provide wind sock in the production facility or installations;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 72 - General safety for production facility and installation
text: provide facility for washing eyes and display material safety data sheets in the chemical handling shed;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 72 - General safety for production facility and installation
text: provide an emergency vehicle in all the time in every production facility or installation;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 72 - General safety for production facility and installation
text: ensure that pipelines are painted as per colour code indicated in the standard, that is, IS 2379 of the Bureau of Indian Standards or its revised version and the said colour code are displayed in the installation;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 72 - General safety for production facility and installation
text: ensure that display boards are posted in strategic locations for prohibitory messages; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 72 - General safety for production facility and installation
text: ensure that all relevant documents pertaining to new production facility regarding construction and material alterations are maintained and kept available at facility.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: acidising operations at a well is carried out under the direct personal supervision of an official authorised for the purpose;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: prior to the acidising operation, all pressure lines and associated equipment are tested to a pressure ten per cent higher than the expected working pressure; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a non-return valve in the treating line as close to the wellhead as practicable is installed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The official shall ensure that -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: no person other than those required for acidising operation remains in the vicinity of the well; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: material safety datasheet is displayed at the site of operation and the requirements of material safety data sheet and personal protective equipment and other protective facilities are provided.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Fracturing operations at a well shall be carried out under the direct personal supervision of an official authorised for the purpose.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Prior to the fracturing operations, discharge pipeline up to the last valve on the wellhead shall be tested to a pressure ten per cent higher than the expected fracturing pressure.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A non-return valve shall be installed in each discharge line as close to the wellhead as practicable.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All discharge and bleed-off lines shall be securely anchored and the bleed off lines shall discharge into open tanks with proper precautions.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The official shall ensure that in the vicinity of the well -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: no person other than those required for fracturing operation remains;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: no naked light or other source of ignition is permitted;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all electrical equipment is de-energised; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate fire-fighting equipment is made available for immediate use.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The pumping units shall be located cross wind at safe distance from the wellhead.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All high pressure pipes shall be anchored and secured.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: A competent person authorised for the purpose shall supervise and check every tanker for its fitness and safe condition, and loading and unloading of the tanker.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: The loading and unloading of tankers carrying petroleum shall not be carried out beyond day light hours or under conditions of lightning, thunder, high winds and heavy rain, except with the permission of the manager of the mine based on job safety analysis and subject to observance of the control measures and other conditions as he may specify.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: A record of every permission referred to in sub-regulation (2) along with sufficient justification shall be maintained by the manager of the mine or other competent person authorised for the purpose.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: All pipe-lines, fittings and delivery hoses or metal pipes, metallic loading arms, swivel joints, tanks, chassis of tankers in the loading and unloading area shall be electrically continuous and be efficiently earthed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: No tanker shall be loaded or unloaded until its engine has been stopped and battery isolated from electrical circuit and the engine shall not be restarted and the battery shall not be connected to the electrical circuit until all tanks, and valves have been securely closed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: No smoking or open flame shall be permitted within thirty metre of the loading point and within the protected area of the loading terminal.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall make adequate arrangement for ventilation in the loading and unloading area to prevent accumulation of flammable vapour near ground level due to low ambient temperature.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: Adequate fire-fighting equipment shall be kept readily available during loading and unloading of tankers for immediate use.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 75 - Precautions during loading and unloading of petroleum tankers
text: There shall be provision for spill containment.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 76 - Effluent Treatment Plant for water produced from wells
text: The effluent treatment plant for water produced from wells shall be designed in such a manner that oil content in each discharge stream meets the stipulation as fixed by the Government of India in the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 76 - Effluent Treatment Plant for water produced from wells
text: The discharge point for water produced from the wells shall be placed in such a manner that such discharges shall not cause harm to the environment.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that every person keeps clear of the wire line between the drum and floor block;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that every person remains at a safe distance from the bailing line during swabbing and scraping operations;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that a master gate valve and tubing hanger are pre-assembled and kept readily available at the well for immediate use in case the well kicks during pulling out or running in tubings;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that no well servicing operation is carried out at any livewell unless proper blowout preventer assembly is securely installed and maintained;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure pressure and function testing of the blowout preventer assembly before commencement of well servicing operation;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that no tubing is pulled out of any well unless the well is properly killed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: provide a properly constructed working platform at the well where artificial lift equipment is to be used unless a submersible pump is used for the purpose;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that repairs, lubrication or greasing are not performed unless the pumping unit is stopped; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that all surface control valves for gas lift, intermittent gas lift or free plunger lift systems are clearly marked for ready identification;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of every mine shall ensure that job safety analysis is conducted before carrying out artificial, secondary or enhanced oil recovery operation and suitable technical and other control measures thereof formulated.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of every mine shall frame and circulate to all persons engaged therein a code of practice.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 79 - Temporary closure of producing well
text: The manager of every mine shall ensure that temporarily closed wells for a period exceeding thirty days are monitored periodically and wherever the wells are not monitored, the following precautions shall be taken, namely:

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 79 - Temporary closure of producing well
text: it shall be filled with mud or water or oil or salt solution or any special chemical so that the hydrostatic pressure of the fluid column over-balances the formation pressure to prevent leakage of petroleum at the wellhead;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 79 - Temporary closure of producing well
text: the control valves of the Christmas tree shall be completely closed and control wheels be removed;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 79 - Temporary closure of producing well
text: the Christmas tree shall be examined for leakage at least once in thirty days by a competent person authorised for the purpose and in case any leakage is detected during such examination, the competent person shall take immediate steps to stop it;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 79 - Temporary closure of producing well
text: a report of every such examination shall be recorded and maintained by the person who made the examination;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 79 - Temporary closure of producing well
text: the well head shall be preferably covered with suitably designed portable protective cover as a security measure.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 80 - Plugging requirements for abandonment of wells
text: When it is intended to abandon a well, the manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 80 - Plugging requirements for abandonment of wells
text: all permeable formations are isolated with cement plug;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 80 - Plugging requirements for abandonment of wells
text: a cement plug of minimum length of fifty metre is placed at the bottom of the well or proper bridge plug;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 80 - Plugging requirements for abandonment of wells
text: a cement plug of a minimum length of fifty metre is placed across the shoes of the surface casing or bridge plug;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 80 - Plugging requirements for abandonment of wells
text: the cellar or pit around the well are filled up and the land shall be restored to the original level; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 80 - Plugging requirements for abandonment of wells
text: a bridge-plug above the top of perforations capped with a thirty metre cement plug is placed in case of cased well.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 80 - Plugging requirements for abandonment of wells
text: Every abandoned well shall be clearly identified at site and on the plan.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

text: CHAPTER –VII TRANSPORT BY PIPELINE

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The regulations in this Chapter shall apply to transportation of petroleum by means of pipelines within any mine referred to in regulation 12.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 82 - Procedure for laying of pipeline
text: No liquid and natural gas transmission pipeline and distribution of piping system shall be laid except in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standards, that is, OISD- STD-141 and OISD-STD-226 or their revised versions.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 82 - Procedure for laying of pipeline
text: While laying a new pipeline, or making any alteration in the existing pipeline in oilfield within any mine, the owner, agent or manager of a mine shall:-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 82 - Procedure for laying of pipeline
text: keep at the mine a surface plan of the area where the pipeline is proposed to be laid showing the extent of land over which right of use has been established and route of the pipeline clearly indicating the districts and states through which the pipeline would pass;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 82 - Procedure for laying of pipeline
text: forward to the concerned railway administration an intimation accompanying a plan where it is proposed to lay pipeline within forty-five metre of any railway in respect of which this regulation is applicable by reason of any general or special order of the Central Government; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 82 - Procedure for laying of pipeline
text: forward to such authority as the Central Government may by a general or special order specify, an intimation accompanying a plan where it is proposed to lay pipeline within forty-five metre of any public works in respect of which this regulation is applicable by reason of any general or special order of the Central Government or of any public road or building or of other permanent structure not belonging to the owner of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 82 - Procedure for laying of pipeline
text: When any pipeline has been commissioned, the owner, agent or manager of the mine shall forthwith submit self-certification in support of complying with the requirements and communicate the actual date of commissioning to the Regional Inspector and a copy thereof be sent to the Chief Inspector of Mines and the District Magistrate.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 82 - Procedure for laying of pipeline
text: The details of all new pipeline laid and any significant alteration in the existing pipelines made during the year in mine shall be maintained and a copy of it shall be forwarded to the Regional Inspector along with annual return.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: the pipeline is designed in a manner that ensures adequate public safety under all conditions likely to be encountered during installation, testing, commissioning and operating conditions;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: all materials and equipment are selected to ensure safety and suitability for the condition of use;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: all pipes, valves, flanges and other fitting conform to the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standards, that is, OISD-STD-141 and OISD-STD-226 or their revised versions;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: all underground pipes and their components are protected against corrosion using suitable external anti-corrosion coating or painting and cathodic protection and all above ground pipes and their components are protected against corrosion by providing suitable anti-corrosion coating;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: the sectionalising valves are installed where required for operation and maintenance and control of emergencies;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: the factors such as topography of the location, ease of operation and maintenance including requirements for section line fill are considered in deciding the location of the valves: Provided that the distance between two consecutive sectionalising valves shall not be more than fifty kilometre in any case.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: the sectionalisation valve is provided based on risk assessment survey to limit the hazard and damage from accidental discharge from pipeline and to facilitate repair and maintenance of pipeline;

text: the pressure safety valves or other devices of sufficient capacity and sensitivity is installed to ensure the normal operating pressure in the pipeline network;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that adequate precaution is taken to protect the pipeline from washout, erosion, land slide, excessive load, impact or other likely hazards, which may cause serious movement or damage to the pipeline.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 83 - Design of pipeline and fittings
text: Where pipeline is laid across the railway or public road, casing pipe shall be extended as specified by the concerned authorities.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 84 - Construction, laying and maintenance of pipeline
text: The pipeline shall be buried with minimum cover as specified in the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD-STD-141 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 84 - Construction, laying and maintenance of pipeline
text: The laying of pipeline shall be planned along a pre-identified surveyed route within right of way or right of use.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 84 - Construction, laying and maintenance of pipeline
text: No pipeline shall be laid unless the land is under the sole control of the owner or right of use has been obtained.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 84 - Construction, laying and maintenance of pipeline
text: The manager of a mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

text: back filling are carried out after the pipeline has been laid in the trench prior to commissioning of the pipeline.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

text: pipeline markers to indicate presence of pipeline and chainage are provided all along the pipeline route.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
