source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall, before the commencement of the commissioning activities of pipelines, ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: commissioning procedure is in place;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: pressure testing is completed for entire pipeline and associated station piping;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: pressure leak check of the above ground piping and flanged joints is completed;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: pipeline has been cleaned and debris has been removed;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: mainline sectionalising valves are installed as per requirement;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: all Golden joints are inspected and accepted;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: geometric survey of pipeline section is carried out, if applicable; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 85 - Preparatory works before commissioning of pipelines
text: trained and experienced officials in adequate numbers are appointed for supervision of commissioning work of pipelines.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 86 - Re-lay, renew or repair of pipeline
text: Where the Chief Inspector of Mines is of the opinion that it is in the interest of public safety so to do, he may, by an order in writing, require the owner, agent or manager of a mine to relay, renew or repair such pipeline in accordance with requirements as may be specified in such order.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 87 - Maintenance of Right of Way or Right of Use and inspection of crossings
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall maintain a periodic pipeline patrol program to observe surface conditions on and adjacent to the pipeline right of way, indication of leaks, construction activity other than that performed by the company, and any other factors affecting the safety and operation of the pipeline.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 87 - Maintenance of Right of Way or Right of Use and inspection of crossings
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall give special attention to activities and features such as road, building, erosion, excavation, encroachment and vegetation growth along the pipeline system.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 87 - Maintenance of Right of Way or Right of Use and inspection of crossings
text: Encroachment, pilferage, theft and other activities of miscreants shall immediately be brought to the notice of district administration and other concerned authorities.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 87 - Maintenance of Right of Way or Right of Use and inspection of crossings
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall maintain right of way or right of use and access to various stations including valve location to ensure reasonable access to maintenance crews and ensuring visibility or availability of pipeline markers.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 88 - Patrolling of Right of Way or Right of Use
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall ensure that ground patrolling of right of way is carried out at least once in a month to observe surface conditions, leakage, any construction activity, encroachments, washouts and any other factors affecting the safety and operation of pipeline.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 88 - Patrolling of Right of Way or Right of Use
text: The manager of the mine shall ensure inspection of the roads and railway crossings at least once in three months by an official authorised for the purpose.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 88 - Patrolling of Right of Way or Right of Use
text: The manager of the mine shall ensure inspection of water body crossings at least twice a year, prior to and after monsoon or flash flood for exposure by an official authorised for the purpose.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 88 - Patrolling of Right of Way or Right of Use
text: The official authorised by the manager shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 88 - Patrolling of Right of Way or Right of Use
text: inspect the rail, road bridge, suspended crossings once in three months to check support and structure and anti-corrosion coating where pipe exits or enters ground;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 88 - Patrolling of Right of Way or Right of Use
text: Inspect right of way or right of use of the mine at least once in a year for the entire length of the pipeline preferably to be done after monsoon;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 88 - Patrolling of Right of Way or Right of Use
text: make the villagers and public along the right of way or right of use adequately aware of the possible consequence of leaks and this shall be included as a part of regular audit; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 88 - Patrolling of Right of Way or Right of Use
text: maintain regular liaison with the police stations, local panchayats and district authorities along the right of way and right of use about the possible consequences of leakage and pilferage.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The manager of every mine shall formulate and implement detailed operation and maintenance procedure or manual for control system and safety interlocks for entire pipeline system of a mine considering the recommendations made of the original equipment manufacturer and based on the local conditions.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The manual or procedure shall include preventive maintenance schedule with periodicity, that is, daily, weekly, monthly, half yearly and yearly and activities to be carried out during each schedule of maintenance.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 95

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The maintenance schedule shall include activities like detecting, isolating and repairing a leak in the pipe line in a safe and efficient manner in accordance with the code of practice developed and approved by the competent authority within the organisation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The manager of a mine shall develop and maintain a standard operating procedure of pipeline.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The manager of a mine shall ensure that the standard operating procedures for integrity assessment of piggable and non-piggable pipelines are followed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The manager of a mine shall ensure that the job safety analysis is carried out for any non-repetitive nature of job.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The manager of a mine shall ensure that work permit system for repair and maintenance work is complied with..

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The manager of a mine shall modify from time to time the plan and procedure of operating practice as the situation warrants in operating conditions through the management of change document, which shall be serially numbered and maintained at the mine with copies at the locations.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The management of change shall include the justification requiring the change of operating conditions and the benefit resulting thereof.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 89 - Maintenance Procedure or Manual of pipeline system
text: The management of change shall be closed with amending the “as built” drawing and the change made in the standard operating procedure along with the completion of the change.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 90 - Pipeline replacement and revalidation policy
text: Every mine shall have a documented policy for repairing, replacement, re-evaluation of safe operating pressure and re-validation of residual life of all pipelines based on recommended practice developed by the owner.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 90 - Pipeline replacement and revalidation policy
text: The recommended practice may be based on the Oil Industry Safety Directorate standards, guidelines, safe operating procedures and applicable international standards.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 91 - Abandonment or discontinuation of pipeline
text: A pipeline system that is no longer required for transportation shall be taken out of service with all hazardous fluid removed from the system.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 91 - Abandonment or discontinuation of pipeline
text: In case the owner, agent or manager of the mine decides to abandon full or part of the pipeline system, he shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 91 - Abandonment or discontinuation of pipeline
text: the pipeline section and facility are disconnected with positive isolation from all sources of supply prior to abandonment; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 91 - Abandonment or discontinuation of pipeline
text: the pipeline system and facility to be abandoned are purged with nitrogen gas, inert materials or inhibited water and after purging, the ends of the pipeline are positively isolated.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 91 - Abandonment or discontinuation of pipeline
text: In case the owner, agent or manager of the mine intends to revive an abandoned or discontinued pipeline, he shall develop and sign the integrity assurance plan before such revival.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of a every mine shall maintain record of design, construction, map, maintenance, and pipeline health monitoring and operations as specified in this Chapter in hard copies or in electronic form at the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Manager of every mine in which any pipeline is laid for transport of petroleum shall submit to the Regional Inspector within sixty days of the coming into force of these regulations or in the case of a new pipeline, within thirty days of the installation of pipeline, comprehensive emergency procedure specifying the action to be taken in the event of fire, uncontrolled escape of petroleum or natural gas from the pipeline, bursting or damage to the pipeline.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of every mine shall maintain the copies of the emergency procedure specific to the pipeline and the station at each control room along with plans and sections and the following records, namely:-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: operational data;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: pipeline patrolling records;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: leak or tapping records;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: routine or unusual inspection records; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: pipeline repair records. CHAPTER-VIII PROTECTION AGAINST GAS AND FIRE

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 94 - Storage and use of flammable material
text: No flammable material shall be stored within thirty metre of any oil well except for fuel in the tanks of the operation equipment: Provided that, where special conditions exist, the manager of the mine may permit such storage within the aforesaid limit in writing based on risk assessment made and subject to observance of the control measures under the charge of an official as he may specify therein.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 94 - Storage and use of flammable material
text: The official specified in sub-regulation(1) shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 94 - Storage and use of flammable material
text: maintain a record of the measures taken by him in respect of the permission referred therein;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 94 - Storage and use of flammable material
text: use safety cans for handling and use of flammable liquid;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 94 - Storage and use of flammable material
text: ensure that drainage from any fuel storage is in a direction away from the well and equipment;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 94 - Storage and use of flammable material
text: Any flammable liquid having a flash point of less than sixty-five degree celsius shall not be used for cleaning purpose except with the prior permission in writing of the manager, deputy manager or an installation manager of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: No person shall enter or be permitted to enter any cellar, sump, pit or any confined space or zone “0” hazardous area or the area where a flare has accidentally extinguished unless it has been examined by a competent person and found to be gas free.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: Where any examination made under sub-regulation (1) shows the concentration of flammable gas in excess of the limit specified for the purpose under the Central Electricity Authority (Measures Relating to Safety and Electric Supply) Regulations, 2010, the supply of electric energy shall be cut off immediately from all cables and apparatus lying within thirty metre of the installation and all sources of ignition shall be removed from the said area and normal work shall not be resumed unless the area is made gas-free.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: The manager of the mine shall provide fixed gas detection system for hydrocarbons and hydrogen sulphide gas, if present, at suitable locations in gas collection station and gas compression plant where there is likelihood of hazard due to gas leakage to continuously monitor the presence of flammable or toxic and to alert persons and initiate action manually or automatically to minimise the probability of exposure of persons and consequences of fire.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: For the production installation other than those specified under sub-regulation (3) the manager of the mine shall take decision for provision of fixed gas detection system based on assessment of risk due to gas leakage and he shall maintain a record of every such decision along with sufficient justification.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: The manager of the mine shall provide portable gas detectors for hydrocarbons and hydrogen sulphide gas, if present, at the installation to ascertain the presence of gases before issuing work permit and for use in areas not covered by fixed gas detection system.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: On detection of hydrogen sulphide gas, every persons on and near the well head, drill floor, shale shaker area, mud pump and tank shall put on suitable escape breathing apparatus of standards, that is, IS 10245 Part 1 or IS 10245 Part 2 of the Bureau of Indian Standards.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: Every person other than those required for control measures shall be withdrawn to a safe area.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: No normal operation shall be resumed unless checks with hydrogen sulphide gas detectors is made and the area has been examined and declared gas free by the installation manager.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: The installation manager shall record and maintain at the installation, the particulars of every occurrence as to where and when the flammable gas was found, when it was removed, and the percentage thereof.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 95 - Precaution against noxious and flammable gas
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 97

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No person shall smoke or be permitted to smoke within thirty metre of any well, separator, petroleum storage tank or other source of flammable gas.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that “no smoking” area in every mine is clearly demarcated.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No naked light or open flame or spark shall be permitted within thirty metre of any well or any place where petroleum is stored except with the permission of the manager as granted under sub-regulation

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: of regulation 94.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No flame type treater, crude oil treater or other flame-type equipment shall be placed or located within thirty metre of any well, separator, petroleum storage tank, except where such flame type equipment is fitted with a flame arrestor.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of every mine shall maintain separation distance between blocks and production facilities in accordance with the Oil Industries Safety Directorates Standard, that is, OISD-STD-118 or its revised version or any other standard specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines by a general or special order based on quantitative risk assessment.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of the mine shall ensure that dead leaves or dry vegetation is not allowed to accumulate or remain, and any combustible material other than that required for use within a period of twenty-four hours is not stored, within a distance of fifteen metre from any oil well or fuel tank storage area.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Where an internal combustion engine is located within thirty metre of any well, separator or storage tank -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: its exhaust pipe shall be insulated or sufficiently cooled and the end of the exhaust pipe shall be directed away from the well head; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: its exhaust manifold shall be shielded to prevent its contact with liquid or gas which might otherwise fall on it.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of the mine shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: provide with an air intake shut-off valve with readily accessible remote control arrangement where a diesel engine is located within thirty metre of a well;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: provide with water bath treater, heater treater and flare line, a suitable device for remote ignition of burners; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure to effectively earth all plant, machinery and derricks for dissipation of any static electric charge.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: No person other than a competent welder duly authorised by the manager shall carry out welding or cutting work requiring use of flame or electric welding apparatus.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: No welder shall undertake any welding or cutting work in any classified hazardous area unless a written permit, called “hot work permit” in the form and method specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines is issued to the supervisor of the welder by the manager, deputy manager or installation manager and copies of every such hot work permit is maintained and kept available at the installation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: The person who has issued the permit shall explain the content of the hot work permit to the concerned welder and take his signature thereon to that effect before the commencement of hot work.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: No work such as welding or cutting or grinding shall be undertaken in hazardous area unless the area is duly examined and found gas free by a competent person authorised for the purpose and a report of every such examination shall be recorded and maintained.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: During any work of welding, cutting or grinding, the welder shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: all flammable material, oil grease, oil-soaked earth are removed from the area;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: no match, lighter, or smoking apparatus or any other source capable of igniting flammable gas is present at or around his place of work:

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to prohibit the use of any suitable apparatus for the purpose of lighting or re-lighting the welding torch;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: adequate precaution is taken to prevent fire being started by spark, slag or hot metal;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: adequate number of suitable type of fire extinguishers and other fire fighting arrangements are made and kept readily available for immediate use;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: when operation is carried out in confined space, adequate ventilation by mechanical means is constantly provided to prevent accumulation of flammable gas; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: when operation is carried out on pipeline which contain flammable fluid, the pipe be disconnected or blinded, the line be positively isolated, drained or purged with inert gas or water before hot work is undertaken and adequate precaution taken against build-up of pressure in the line while hot work is in progress: Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to prohibit the use of hot-tapping machine on a running pipeline with prior written permission of the manager, deputy manager or an installation manager.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 98 - Precautions during welding, cutting or grinding
text: The installation manager shall ensure that where hot work permit is issued, welding and cutting operations are carried out in accordance with the said permits.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 99 - Permit to work system for electrical installations
text: The work on major electrical installations shall be carried out under “Permit to Work System” in accordance with the standard, that is, IS 5216 Part 1 of the Bureau of Indian Standards or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager of every mine shall ensure that fire-fighting facility provided under these regulations conforms to the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standards, that is, OISD- STD-116, OISD-STD-117 and OISD-STD-189 or their revised versions.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall provide the following fire fighting arrangements and equipment at every drilling and work-over rig, namely:-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate water storage, pumping facility, hose and nozzle; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate number of appropriate portable fire extinguishers at the derrick floor, main engine area, electrical machinery, mud tank area, diesel storage area and other vulnerable places.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall provide fire fighting arrangement and equipment at every production facility in accordance with the standards specified in sub-regulation (1).

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall provide a water ring main with adequate storage of water at site, pump feeding hydrants and water monitors at every petroleum storage tank.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: provide fixed-roof storage tanks with fixed foam connections;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that all installations, including well head area with Christmas tree, are easily accessible to facilitate external fire fighting assistance without any hindrance; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ensure that adequate number of fire tenders and equipment are kept readily available at convenient locations for use in emergency.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A fire officer shall once in every three months examine every fire extinguisher and discharge and refill it as often as may be necessary to ensure that it is in proper working order.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The fire officer shall record and maintain a report of every such examination or refilling under this regulation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that every person employed at any drilling rig, work-over rig, production facility, well head installation, storage tank or on such other work where fire-fighting equipment may be required to be used, are trained in use of such equipment and regular fire drills are held for the purpose.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 99

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of every mine shall frame an emergency plan for implementation in the event of an emergency and submit a copy thereof to the Regional Inspector and District Magistrate.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The emergency plan referred to in sub-regulation (1) shall be prepared after carrying out risk assessment of the activities in the mines with respect to -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: fire;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: blowout, explosion, ignition, influx of inflammable or noxious gas;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: bursting of equipment, pipeline or uncontrolled escape of petroleum;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: failure of structures;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: chemical spillage;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: natural calamities;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: medical evacuation; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: any other emergencies.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The emergency plan shall contain-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the action to be taken in the event of any major accident including when and how the said action is to be taken;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: organisation plan clearly stating the line of command and the responsibilities of each person involved in case of emergency situation;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: equipment plan such as the make, type, capacity, location, field of operation, and operating procedure in respect of every equipment; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: strategy plan specifying the number of steps to be taken in any particular case of emergency.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The emergency plan shall clearly stipulate –

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: alarm and communication system;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: system of notifying the authorities;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the duties and responsibilities of each key personnel including measures to be adopted to avert or minimise the consequences of the emergency;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: when and how the equipment shall be used and when and how the action shall be carried out;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: help or information that would be available from associated and external agencies including Government agencies;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: guidelines for terminating the action; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: plan for training of personnel and for mock-drills.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all equipment on the installation provided for compliance with the emergency plan is maintained in a reliable state under efficient working order and in good condition without repair; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: there is a written scheme prepared for the systematic examination of all emergency equipment by an independent competent person.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: specify the nature and frequency of examination that may be carried out, before first use of the equipment on the installation and also after any modification or repair; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: review and modify the emergency plan periodically and in particular before any alteration is carried out and submit a copy thereof to the Regional Inspector.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: CHAPTER- IX MACHINERY, PLANT AND EQUIPMENT

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 103 - Use of certain machinery and equipment
text: The owner, agent and manager of every mine shall ensure that-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 103 - Use of certain machinery and equipment
text: the appliance, equipment, machinery or other material that are used or may be used in the mine are of sound construction and of a type and specification conforming to an Indian or international standard identified for the purpose: Provided that such appliance, equipment, machinery or other material shall not be used in the mine unless the same has been tested and passed the test as per the standard and the manager has kept a record of the type, details of specification, reference of the particular standard, test criteria in accordance with the standard and status of testing, place of testing, copies of test report and any other relevant details; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 103 - Use of certain machinery and equipment
text: the code of practice and standard operating procedure for appliance, equipment, machinery or other material are complied with.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The mobile crane shall not be used in mines for lifting loads beyond its rated capacity.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The mobile cranes used in mines shall be provided with-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a safe load indicator to give warning when overload occurs;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a load radius indicator to indicate appropriate safe working load and radius which is clearly visible to the operator of the crane;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: motion limit device to limit hoisting, swinging and boom extending; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: effective audio visual alarm which shall get activated automatically whenever the crane is reversed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The operator of the crane shall have a clear view of the load being handled and where it is not practicable to do so, suitable signaling system shall be provided for giving signals for safe operations.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The slings, as per the standard, that is, IS 2762 of the Bureau of Indian Standards or its revised version shall only be used with the mobile crane.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall permit only competent and trained operator of the crane to operate the mobile crane in the mines.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The mobile crane shall be examined by a competent person at such interval and in accordance with such procedure as laid down by the manager or deputy manager and the result of such inspection shall be recorded by the person making the inspection.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that lifting appliance and gear system intended to be used in the mine is properly maintained and operated for safety of the person.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The competent person authorised to operate the lifting appliance and gear in a mine shall ensure that -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the design, construction and installation of lifting appliance, gear and rope are in accordance with the standard specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines by a general or special order in writing; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the selection of appliance and gear are suitable for the purpose for which they are intended for use and shall also be load tested for its rated capacity.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that job related training has been imparted to any person before he is engaged to operate the lifting appliances or gears in a mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The hazardous area in an oil mine shall be classified into different zones as specified in the Schedule.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that –

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 101

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a plan referred to in clause (b) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 9 and also the different classes of hazardous zones of the mine, as classified under sub-regulation (1), shown in distinct colour wash, is maintained;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: different classes of zones are distinctly marked at actual places in the field by suitable notice boards or other effective means; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: every person employed in the mine is made aware of the dangers associated and precautions to be taken in case of different classified hazardous zones.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 107 - Use of electrical equipment in hazardous area
text: No person shall use electrical appliance, equipment or machinery including lighting apparatus in zone “0” hazardous area.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 107 - Use of electrical equipment in hazardous area
text: The owner, agent and manager of every mine shall ensure that the electrical appliance, equipment, machinery or other material that are used or may be used in zone “1” or zone “2” hazardous area of the mine is of a type and specification conforming to an Indian standard or an international standard adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards through harmonisation, specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines by a general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided that where no such standard exists, the Chief Inspector of Mines on merit, by a general order notified in the Official Gazette may accept an international standard: Provided further that the Chief Inspector of Mines on merit, by a general order notified in the Official Gazette may accept any other international standard equivalent to the harmonised standard: Provided also that such appliance, equipment, machinery or other material shall not be used in zone “1” and zone “2” hazardous area of the mine unless the same has been tested and passed the test as per the applicable standard and the manager has kept a record of the type, details of specification, reference of the particular standard, test criteria as per the standards and status of testing, place of testing, copies of test reports and any other relevant details.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 108 - General provisions about construction and maintenance of machinery
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that all parts and working gear whether fixed or moveable including the anchoring and apparatus used as or forming part of the equipment of a mine and all foundations in or to which any such appliance is anchored or fixed are of good construction, suitable material, adequate strength and free from visible defect and shall be properly maintained.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure that all machinery and equipment in the mine are maintained in an efficient working order and kept in good repair.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that a procedure is established for the maintenance of machinery and equipment.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The procedure referred to in sub-regulation (2) shall -

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: specify the nature and frequency of examination; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: provide for an examination to be carried out, where appropriate, before the machinery and equipment is first used on the installation and also after major modification or repair.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The examination referred to in sub-regulation (3) shall mean critical scrutiny of machinery and equipment, in or out of service, using suitable techniques, including testing-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: to assess its suitability for the purpose for which these are to be used;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: to assess its actual condition; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: to determine any remedial measures.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The procedure established under this regulation shall include a plan for implementation of the remedial measures identified.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All measuring gadgets, meters and relief valves shall be calibrated at intervals specified by the manufacturers.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that internal combustion engine of over thirty horse power is provided with means other than manual, for starting them: Provided that nothing in this sub-regulation shall be deemed to prohibit manual starting in an emergency.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Where compressed air is used for starting the engine, a non-return valve shall be provided in the compressed airline as close to the engine as practicable.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The exhaust system of the engine shall be provided with suitable device to prevent discharge of open flame and sparks from the exhaust.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Adequate precaution shall be taken to prevent accumulation of flammable vapour near the internal combustion engine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The electrical accessories of an internal combustion engine, if installed in hazardous area shall comply with the provisions of the regulation 107.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Standard, that is, OISD–STD-128 or its revised version is followed for operation and maintenance of apparatus under pressure.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All apparatus used as or forming part of the equipment of a mine which contains or produces air, gas, petroleum or steam at a pressure greater than atmospheric pressure shall be so constructed, installed and maintained as to obviate any risk of fire, bursting, explosion or collapse or the production of noxious gas.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every air receiver or container or separator used for storage of petroleum or gas or steam under pressure shall be fitted with a safety valve and pressure measuring device which shows pressure in excess of the atmospheric pressure.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A competent person shall, before an air receiver or a container containing petroleum or gas or steam is cased in or put in commission-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: subject it to a hydraulic test at a pressure at least one and a half times of the maximum permissible working pressure and similar test shall be made after every renewal or repair and in any case at intervals of not more than five years or at such shorter intervals as may be required by the Regional Inspector; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: record the result of every such test which shall be countersigned and dated by the manager, deputy manager or installation manager.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that the discharge line of an apparatus under pressure is provided with a pressure relieving safety device.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: There shall be no valve or fitting between the pressure apparatus and its pressure relieving safety device or between the device and point of discharge, as would render the device ineffective.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The pressure relieving safety device shall be set to open at a pressure not exceeding ten per cent above the maximum allowable working pressure.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The pressure relieving safety device shall be tested and calibrated at least once in a year and record of such test shall be maintained at the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every incoming gas line connected to any compressor shall be provided with a shut-off valve at a distance outside the compressor shed.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No repair shall be undertaken in respect of any gas compressor and pipelines and fittings connected to it unless the control valve of the inlet and discharge lines are closed and securely locked.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 112 - Precautions regarding moving parts of machinery
text: In every mine, where winch is used, it shall be provided and used with a stopper, pawl or other reliable holder.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 112 - Precautions regarding moving parts of machinery
text: Every flywheel and every other dangerous exposed part of any machinery used as or forming part of the equipment shall be adequately fenced by suitable guards of substantial construction to prevent

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 112 - Precautions regarding moving parts of machinery
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 103 danger and such guards shall be kept in position while the parts of the machinery are in motion or in use but they may be removed for carrying out any examination, adjustment or repair if adequate precaution is taken.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 112 - Precautions regarding moving parts of machinery
text: No person shall be allowed to repair, adjust, clean or lubricate machinery in motion where there is risk of injury.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 112 - Precautions regarding moving parts of machinery
text: No person shall be allowed to shift or adjust a driving belt, chain or rope while the machinery is in motion unless a proper mechanical appliance is provided for the purpose.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 112 - Precautions regarding moving parts of machinery
text: No person in close proximity to moving machinery shall wear or be permitted to wear loose outer clothing.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 112 - Precautions regarding moving parts of machinery
text: No unauthorised person shall be permitted to enter in any engine room, including gas turbine, compressor, or other machine area, or in any way interfere with the machinery.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every engine, motor, compressor, turbine and pump room, and every room in which highly flammable materials are stored shall be kept clean, and be provided with at least two exits.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every exit shall be clearly marked, properly maintained and kept free from obstruction.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 114 - Working and examination of machinery
text: No machinery shall be operated by any person, otherwise than by or under the constant supervision of a competent person.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 114 - Working and examination of machinery
text: Every person in charge of a machinery, apparatus or appliance shall before commencing work ensure that it is in proper working order and if he observes any defect therein, he shall immediately report the fact to the installation manager or other competent person.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 114 - Working and examination of machinery
text: Every person in-charge of an air-receiver shall ensure that no extra weight is added to the safety valves and that the permissible pressure of the air is not exceeded.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 114 - Working and examination of machinery
text: A competent person or persons appointed for the purpose shall, once at least in every seven days, make an inspection of all machinery and plant in use, and shall record the result thereof.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 114 - Working and examination of machinery
text: The electrical machinery and plant shall be under the charge of competent person holding a valid electrical supervisor’s certificate of competency, covering mining installation issued under the Central Electricity Authority (Measures Relating to Safety and Electric Supply) Regulations, 2010. CHAPTER - X GENERAL SAFETY PROVISION

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall, before introducing any machinery or new operation connected with the mine, frame and enforce code of practice, not being inconsistent with the Act or these regulations, for each such machinery or operation, as the case may be.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The code of practice shall be framed with due regard to the type, size and capacity of the machinery or operation in use and prevailing local conditions and a copy of the same shall be submitted to the Regional Inspector, who may at any time, by an order in writing, require such modifications in the code as he may specify therein: Provided that in a mine where such machinery or operation is already in use, the aforesaid code of practice shall be framed and enforced within ninety days from the date of coming into force of these regulations.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The code of practice, inter-alia, shall provide for–

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: safe operating procedures for the machinery or operation to which it relates to;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: examination and testing of the machinery before first use after installation, re-installation, modification, alteration or repair;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: schedule and nature of examination and testing of the machine, including its sub-assemblies, so as to ensure its safe operation; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the manner in which the record of examination shall be kept.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall hand over copies of such code of practice to concerned officials and ensure effective enforcement thereof.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A copy of the code of practice referred to in sub-regulation (1) shall always be kept in the office of the mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The loose materials, which are not required for use, shall not be placed or left so as to dangerously obstruct the workplace and passage-way.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All projecting nails and ends of railings shall be bent or covered with protection to prevent injury.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The scrap, waste and rubbish shall not be allowed to accumulate in work places, access or egress.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Workplace and passage-way that are slippery owing to oil, mud or other causes shall be cleaned up and made safe.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The portable equipment shall be returned after use to its designated storage place.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The equipment, tools and small objects shall not be left lying about where they could cause an accident either by falling or causing person to trip.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure provision of adequate general lighting arrangements during working hours at the following places, namely:-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: where the natural lighting is insufficient;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: derrick floor;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: driller’s stand and control panel;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: monkey board;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: every engine and pump house;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: derrick sub-structure near blowout preventer controls;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: every place where persons are to work; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: every means of escape, access or egress.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The lighting provided in a mine shall as far as possible be so arranged as to prevent glare or eye strain.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every electrical lighting apparatus used in hazardous area of a mine shall be of such type and specifications conforming to an Indian standard or an international standard adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards through harmonisation, specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines by a general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided that where no such standard exists, the Chief Inspector of Mines may on merit, accept an international standard specified by him by a general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided further that the Chief Inspector of Mines may accept any other international standard equivalent to the harmonised standard specified by him by a general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided also that such apparatus shall not be used in hazardous area of the mine unless the same has been tested and passed the tests as per the applicable standards and the manager has kept a record of the type, details of specification, reference of the particular standard, test criteria in accordance with the standards and status of testing, place of testing, copies of test report and any other relevant details.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The lighting system installed in the mine shall comply with the provisions of the Central Electricity Authority (Measures Relating to Safety and Electric Supply) Regulations, 2010.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of every mine shall ensure that the standard of lighting in any specified area or place in a mine is in accordance with the Oil Industry Safety Directorate Recommended Practices, that is, OISD-RP -149 or its revised version.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: ¹Hkkx II μ[k.M 3 (i) º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 105

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In every mine adequate number of self contained portable hand lamps of the standard specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines, by a general or special order in writing, shall be kept available for immediate use in emergency.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Supply and use of protective footwear
text: No person shall go into work or be allowed to go into work in a mine unless he wears a protective footwear of a type and specification conforming to an Indian standard or an international standard adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards through harmonisation, specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines by a general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided that where no such standard exists, the Chief Inspector of Mines may on merit specify an international standard, by a general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided further that the Chief Inspector of Mines may, on merit, by a general order notified in the Official Gazette, specify any other international standard equivalent to the harmonised standard: Provided also that such footwear shall not be used in the mine unless the same has been tested and passed the test as per the applicable standard and the manager has kept a record of the type, details of specification, reference of the particular standard, test criteria as per the standard and status of testing, place of testing, copies of test report and any other relevant details.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Supply and use of protective footwear
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure supply of protective footwear referred to in sub-regulation (1) at interval not exceeding one year or such other intervals as the Chief Inspector of Mines may specify by a general or special order in writing: Provided that where such foot wear is damaged during its legitimate use, it shall be immediately replaced free of cost.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Supply and use of protective footwear
text: The owner, agent or manager of a mine shall provide protective footwear free of charge.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 122 - Supply and use of protective helmet
text: No person shall go into, or work, or be allowed to go into work in a drilling rig or work-over rig or rig building or rig dismantling or at such other place of work where there is a hazard from flying or falling objects unless he wears a helmet of a type and specification conforming to an Indian standard or an international standard adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards through harmonisation, specified by the Chief Inspector of Mines by a general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided that where no such standard exists, the Chief Inspector of Mines may on merit specify an international standard by general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided further that the Chief Inspector of Mines may on merit specify any other international standard equivalent to the harmonised standard by general order notified in the Official Gazette: Provided also that such helmets shall not be used in the mine unless the same has been tested and passed the test as per the applicable standards and the manager has kept a record of the type, details of specification, reference of the particular standards, test criteria as per the standards and status of testing, place of testing, copies of test report and any other relevant details.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 122 - Supply and use of protective helmet
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure supply of helmet referred to in sub-regulation

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 122 - Supply and use of protective helmet
text: at intervals not exceeding three years and shall at all times maintain a sufficient stock of helmets in order to ensure immediate supply as and when need for the same arise: Provided that when a helmet is damaged during its legitimate use, it shall be immediately replaced.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 122 - Supply and use of protective helmet
text: The owner, agent or manager of mine shall provide protective helmet free of charge.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every person engaged in the operation and every other person who may be exposed to the risk of injury, poisoning or disease arising from the operations shall be provided with-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: depending upon the risk, suitable protective equipment including respiratory protective equipment, eye protectors, gloves, overalls and aprons; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: suitable protective outer clothing for use in rain and extreme weather conditions.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure supply of the protective equipment under sub- regulation (1) free of charge.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every person provided with protective equipment shall use the same while at work.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager shall take steps to reduce the noise level and to reduce the exposure of work persons to noise.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No person shall be allowed to work without appropriate ear protection in an area if he is exposed to an equivalent continuous noise level exceeding 90 dB(A).

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No person shall enter or be allowed to enter an area in which the sound level is 140 dB(A) or more.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Chief Inspector of Mines may, from time to time, by notification in the Official Gazette, specify the permissible noise exposure in any area or place in a mine.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner, agent or manager of the mine shall provide and maintain efficient means of communication and an alternative means of signalling in good working order between manned installations including the office of the manager and other places of work and wherever possible, provide the communication by radio telephone.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The equipment for external communication shall be chosen on the basis of operational needs, type of activity and defined situations of hazard and accident.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The communication equipment and associated power supply shall be designed and protected so as to remain functional in situations of hazard or accident.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Where electrical signalling is used-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate precautions shall be taken to prevent signal and telephone wires coming into contact with other cables and apparatus;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the voltage used in any one circuit shall not exceed thirty volts;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: contact-makers shall be so constructed as to prevent accidental closing of the circuit; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: bare conductors, where used, shall be installed in suitable insulators.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 126 - Safety while working at height
text: Where any person is working at more than one metre and eighty centimetre height and where there is likelihood of fall hazard, the owner, agent or manager of mine shall provide a safety belt with full body harness as per the standard, that is, IS 3521 of the Bureau of Indian Standards or its revised version which shall be attached by means of a lifeline to a fixed anchor.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 127 - Precautions against dust, toxic gases and ionizing radiations
text: The owner, agent and manager of a mine shall ensure the prevention and control of emission of dust, toxic gas, fume and ionising radiation at source as far as reasonably practicable.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 127 - Precautions against dust, toxic gases and ionizing radiations
text: A competent person shall instruct every person liable to be exposed to dust, toxic gas, fume and ionising radiation about the safe working method and technique.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 127 - Precautions against dust, toxic gases and ionizing radiations
text: The permissible limit of exposure to dust, toxic gas, fume and ionising radiation shall be as per the applicable regulatory requirements.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The storage area and container of toxic, corrosive, flammable, poisonous and radioactive material shall be properly labelled and appropriately stored according to material safety data sheet content.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The warning sign shall be posted to denote any hazardous situation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The warning sign shall be posted in area where the use of personal protective equipment is required.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The identification sign shall be clearly posted to locate emergency equipment and direction of escape route.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The manager shall ensure that the pipelines carrying steam or fluid at high pressure are clearly identified.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: The owner, agent and manage of every mine shall comply with the standards and guidelines of the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986 for discharging liquid effluent and gaseous emission, and for disposal of solid waste, drill cutting and drilling fluid applicable for oil drilling and gas extraction industry.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: Any oil discharged from a well during its completion, testing and repair shall be collected in suitably constructed and adequately fenced disposal pits or tanks suitably located.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: No disposal pit shall be constructed within forty-five metre of any railway, public road or of any public works or of other permanent structure not belonging to the owner.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: The untreated sewage, formation water, oil, drilling fluid, waste, chemical substances or refuse from a well, tank or other production installation shall not be permitted,-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

text: to create hazard to public health and safety;

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: to run into or contaminate any fresh water structure or body of water or to remain in a place from which it might contaminate any fresh water or body of water; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

text: to run over or damage any land, highway or public road.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: No fluid shall be discharged into sea or fresh water structure or any body of water, unless it is treated and samples of fluid at regular interval are obtained and analysed and the result of such analysis is maintained by the person collecting the sample and analysing the same.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: No scrap, surplus or unused material shall be permitted to be dumped or disposed off in the sea or in the vicinity of any installation.

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: The gas produced at any installation shall not be discharged to the atmosphere unless burnt in accordance with clause (b); and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

text: the gas to be burnt, referred to in clause (a) shall be discharged from a flare line in the following manners, namely:-

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines

text: flare-stack height shall be in accordance with the standards referred under sub-regulation (1);

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Protection against pollution of environment
text: the flare-line shall be adequately anchored and provided with suitable means to prevent extinction of the flame; and

source_framework: Oil Mining - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
